chunks.text: Yi Ma is a Visiting Professor at UC Berkeley's EECS department, with contact information including office location at 333A Cory Hall, phone number (510) 664 4565, and email yima@eecs.berkeley.edu. Office hours are by appointment at 333A Cory Hall. Administrative support is provided by Sarah Lirio at 353 Cory Hall with email sarah.lirio@berkeley.edu.

Yi Ma earned his B.S. in Automation and Applied Mathematics from Tsinghua University in 1995, followed by M.S. in EECS (1997), M.A. in Mathematics (2000), and Ph.D. in EECS (2000) from UC Berkeley. He was faculty at University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign (2000-2011), managed Visual Computing Group at Microsoft Research Asia (2009-2013), served as founding professor and executive dean at ShanghaiTech University (2014-2017), and joined UC Berkeley EECS faculty in 2018.

Yi Ma's research areas include Artificial Intelligence (AI), Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), and Signal Processing (SP), with specific focus on Computer Vision and Compressive Sensing. He is affiliated with research centers: Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR), Center for Augmented Cognition (CAC), and FHL Vive Center for Enhanced Reality.

Selected publications include 'Generalized Principal Component Analysis' (2016), 'Robust Principal Component Analysis' (2011), 'Robust Face Recognition via Sparse Representation' (2009), and 'An Invitation to 3D Vision: From Images to Models' (2004). Honors include SIAM Fellow (2020), ACM Fellow (2017), IEEE Fellow (2013), ONR Young Investigator (2005), NSF CAREER Award (2004), and CS TCPAMI ICCV Best Paper Award/David Marr Prize (1999).
